title: casearia engleri
intro: Casearia engleri, also known as the Guianese casearia, is a plant species native to the Guiana Shield region in South America, belonging to the family Salicaceae. Traditionally, Casearia engleri has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Casearia engleri can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the latter being a popular herbal preparation used to harness its medicinal properties.
